title: Character Set Packing Codes
text: Character Set Packing Codes The C packing code is used for characters in Uniface installable character sets. Each entity has a character set used for storing the entity in the database. By default, this is the character set selected during installation, but it can also be a character set specified in the Character Set of the Entity's Database Interface , or using the $DEF_CHARSET assignment setting (if Character Set = (default) . Fields with a String data type can contain characters in the entity character set. They can have the following packing codes: C or C n packing code C n means that the maximum number of characters is n . However, if this String is used for holding double-byte characters, only n / 2 characters can be held. Therefore C1 cannot be used for double-byte characters. On a Windows platform, any characters can be entered into a C String field, but characters that do not belong to the entity character set cause field validation to fail. If the Wide Character Behavior property of the Entity's Database Interface is set to Character Set , the W packing code becomes a C packing code. Note: iSeries systems predominantly use the DB2 database.For more information, see DB2 Data Packing . U n packing code The U* packing code can contain characters in its entity character set if the $META_IN_TRX assignment setting is set to 1 . For more information, see $META_IN_TRX . Related Links Character Packing Code (C) The C packing code is used for String data types. TRX Character Packing Codes (U, VU, and SU) The U, VU, and SU packing codes should be specified for any data that is to be stored in the Uniface transfer format (TRX). TRX has not been used since Uniface 8, so these packing codes are only available to ensure compatibility with older applications. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
